cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. The question is asking why Doyle was surprised. The options are A to D. So, the story says that Doyle got into a taxi, and the driver immediately addressed him by his name, Mr. Doyle. Doyle was surprised and asked if the driver had seen him before. The driver said no, then explained how he deduced that Doyle was Arthur Conan Doyle based on various clues: the newspaper story about his vacation, the taxi stop, his skin color, ink spot on fingers, and his English clothes. Finally, the driver mentions the name on the suitcase. The key point here is that the driver called him by his name before Doyle even said anything. That's why Doyle was surprised. The driver hadn't seen him before, so he shouldn't have known his name. The driver explained how he figured it out, but initially, the surprise was because the driver knew his name without being told. So looking at the options: A) the driver had seen him before – but the driver said he hadn't, so probably not. B) the driver could call him by his name – yes, that's exactly what happened.
